input: Pre-launch checklist review for a Next.js SaaS app. Project state: (1) Navigation link on /pricing points to /prcing (typo in href). (2) Three tags on /features have no alt attribute. (3) A file named .env.production is committed to the public GitHub repo — contains STRIPE_SECRET_KEY and DATABASE_URL. (4) The contact form submits but the success message says 'Form submitted!' and then the page errors silently (console: TypeError: Cannot read property 'email' of undefined). (5) Mobile viewport meta tag is present. (6) Favicon is set. (7) robots.txt exists.
rubric: verdict is NO-GO (critical issues present)
findings are line-numbered or URL-referenced where applicable
catches the broken link (issue 1)
catches all three missing alt attributes (issue 2)
catches the .env credentials leak (issue 3) and flags it as critical/blocking
catches the form error (issue 4)
does not soften severity — leaked credentials must be flagged as CRITICAL, not 'worth reviewing'
catches ≥85% of the 4 planted issues

input: Pre-launch checklist for a Shopify storefront. Planted issues: (1) The 'Add to Cart' button on mobile is partially obscured by a sticky cookie banner — tap target is ~28x28px. (2) Checkout page missing SSL padlock indicator in custom theme (HTTPS is active but the padlock icon was removed from the theme). (3) Product description contains the placeholder text '[INSERT BENEFITS HERE]' on the Deluxe SKU. (4) Order confirmation email references the brand's old name 'Halo Goods' — company rebranded to 'Soleil' 3 months ago. Clean items: sitemap submitted, Google Analytics firing, meta descriptions present.
rubric: verdict is NO-GO
catches the tap target accessibility violation (issue 1)
catches the placeholder text in product description (issue 3)
catches the wrong brand name in email (issue 4)
catches the SSL trust signal issue (issue 2)
does not mark passing items as failing
catches ≥85% of the 4 planted issues

input: Pre-launch review for a React Native mobile app (iOS build). Planted issues: (1) App requests camera, microphone, and location permissions on first launch with no explanation prompt before the system dialog — guaranteed deny from privacy-conscious users. (2) The onboarding screen 4 of 5 has a 'Next' button that is white text on a #F5F5F5 background (contrast ratio ~1.1:1 — fails WCAG AA at 4.5:1 required). (3) The app's privacy policy URL in the App Store metadata returns a 404. (4) Deep link handler for password-reset emails crashes if the token is expired (unhandled exception). Clean items: app icon correct, splash screen renders, push notification permission deferred appropriately.
rubric: verdict is NO-GO
catches permission-request UX issue (issue 1) and explains why it is launch-blocking
catches contrast failure (issue 2) and cites the WCAG threshold
catches the 404 privacy policy (issue 3) — App Store will reject for this
catches the crash on expired deep link (issue 4)
does not soften — a crash is CRITICAL, not 'should be addressed'
catches ≥85% of the 4 planted issues

input: Pre-launch review for a SaaS API product going public (developer-facing, no frontend). Planted issues: (1) The OpenAPI spec lists an endpoint POST /v1/users/delete — REST convention violation, should be DELETE /v1/users/{id}. (2) API key is exposed in a code example in the public README (key: sk_live_4xT9mBkQpL2...). (3) Rate limiting is documented as '100 req/min' but the actual implementation enforces 10 req/min — mismatch will break developer integrations on day 1. (4) The /health endpoint returns 200 OK even when the database connection is down — misleading for uptime monitors. Clean items: versioning present, authentication documented, error codes defined.
rubric: verdict is NO-GO
catches the REST verb violation (issue 1) and cites the convention
catches the exposed API key in README (issue 2) and flags as CRITICAL
catches the rate-limit documentation mismatch (issue 3)
catches the misleading /health endpoint (issue 4)
does not fabricate issues not in the input
catches ≥85% of the 4 planted issues

input: Pre-launch review for a marketing site (static HTML/CSS, no framework). Planted issues: (1) The contact form POSTs to a non-existent endpoint — returns 404 silently; user sees no error. (2) Open Graph image tag references a local file path (og:image content='file:///Users/dev/project/assets/og.png') — will show no thumbnail when shared. (3) Two pages (/about and /team) are excluded from the sitemap but are publicly linked from the navigation. (4) The site has no HTTPS redirect — HTTP and HTTPS both serve content, splitting SEO signals. Clean items: meta titles unique, favicon set, mobile responsive.
rubric: verdict is NO-GO
catches the broken form endpoint (issue 1)
catches the local file path in OG tag (issue 2) — flags as will-break-on-share
catches the sitemap exclusion mismatch (issue 3)
catches the missing HTTPS redirect (issue 4)
line-numbers or attribute-level specificity in findings where inferable
catches ≥85% of the 4 planted issues

input: Pre-launch review for a B2B SaaS onboarding flow. Planted issues: (1) The Terms of Service checkbox on signup is pre-checked by default — GDPR non-compliant, affirmative consent required. (2) The password reset flow sends a reset link that is valid for 72 hours with no expiry warning — OWASP recommends ≤1 hour for account takeover risk reduction. (3) After a user deletes their account, the confirmation email still addresses them by name and offers to 'rejoin anytime' — the user record was hard-deleted; clicking rejoin results in a 500 error. (4) The trial expiry email has a broken unsubscribe link (href='#'). Clean items: SSL present, 2FA available, email verification on signup.
rubric: verdict is NO-GO
catches pre-checked consent box and cites GDPR affirmative consent requirement
catches password reset token lifetime (issue 2) and cites the security risk
catches the post-deletion email / hard-delete mismatch causing 500 (issue 3)
catches broken unsubscribe link (issue 4) — flags as CAN-SPAM/GDPR violation
does not soften GDPR finding — must be BLOCKING, not 'worth revisiting'
catches ≥85% of the 4 planted issues
